food_subgroup: Processed seafood snacks / deep-fried prawn-based crackers

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03302050 covers shrimp cracker as a category; no exact entry for 'crispy prawn cracker' as a distinct deep-fried variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers crustacean-based snack products; no exact code for crispy prawn cracker as a distinct hot-pot accompaniment. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for crispy prawn cracker as a food product at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard retail pack of crispy prawn crackers weighs 200 g, as described in the GrabFood listing; a typical single-occasion portion for hot-pot or snacking is approximately 30–50 g.

nutrient_source.title: Prawn crackers, fried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 35188) and USDA Branded Food cross-reference for deep-fried shrimp/prawn cracker snacks
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry for 'crispy prawn cracker' as a distinct product was available. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for fried prawn/shrimp crackers (NDB 35188 and related entries) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for commercially fried prawn cracker snacks. Values represent a composite estimate for a deep-fried tapioca-and-prawn cracker product. Per-100 g values are consistent with published ranges for this food category.

gi_source.title: Prawn crackers (krupuk udang), GI value from Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC — International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (American Diabetes Association peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 70 is derived from the International Tables of Glycemic Index (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) for prawn crackers / krupuk udang, which are tapioca-starch-based deep-fried cracker products directly analogous to crispy prawn crackers. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 63.0 × 0.30) / 100 ≈ 13.2 for a 30 g portion (18.9 g carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (620 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should strictly limit crispy prawn cracker consumption, as a 100 g portion provides approximately 27% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg, and typical snacking portions can rapidly accumulate sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 70) and high refined carbohydrate content (63 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit crispy prawn crackers due to their high GI of 70 and dense refined carbohydrate load, which can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, portion size should be kept to no more than 20–30 g and paired with low-GI foods.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (11.5 g per 100 g), high sodium (620 mg per 100 g), and deep-fried preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of crispy prawn crackers, as the combination of high saturated fat from deep-frying, elevated sodium, and trace trans fat presents multiple cardiovascular risk factors; occasional consumption of a small portion (≤30 g) is preferable to regular snacking.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crispy Prawn Cracker (香脆虾签, Xiāng cuì xiā qiān) is a deep-fried seafood snack popular in Singapore and across Southeast Asia, commonly served as a hot-pot accompaniment or standalone snack. The cracker is made from a dough of tapioca starch, wheat flour, prawn or shrimp paste, salt, and sugar, which is shaped, dried, and then deep-fried in vegetable oil until it puffs up into a light, airy, and intensely crunchy wafer. The texture is characteristically crisp and fragile, with a savoury umami-forward prawn flavour and a mild sweetness. In Singapore, it is frequently sold in 200 g retail packs and appears on hot-pot menus as a dipping or side item. Nutritionally, crispy prawn crackers are energy-dense at approximately 498 kcal per 100 g, driven by their high refined carbohydrate content (63 g per 100 g) and significant fat content from deep-frying (25 g per 100 g, of which 11.5 g is saturated fat). Sodium is elevated at 620 mg per 100 g. The glycemic index is high (GI 70), making this food a concern for blood glucose management. Clinically, this snack is not recommended for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium, high GI, and high saturated fat profile. It is halal-certified in most commercial preparations but contains crustacean and gluten allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in prawn crackers?
What is the GI of prawn crackers?
Is keropok udang the same as prawn cracker?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry was found for crispy prawn cracker as a distinct product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Branded Food entries for fried prawn/shrimp crackers, which are directly analogous products. GI is sourced from the peer-reviewed International Tables of Glycemic Index (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) for krupuk udang / prawn crackers. Confidence is medium rather than high because the nutrient values are adapted rather than exact matches from a primary regional source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 35188 and related fried prawn cracker entries) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food data. No exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry was available for this specific product. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 3. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for fried prawn crackers. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for fried prawn crackers. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for fried prawn crackers. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for fried prawn crackers. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for fried prawn crackers. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for fried prawn crackers. 9. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero — deep-fried starch-based snack contains no meaningful vitamin C; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this food category. 10. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index, Diabetes Care, for prawn crackers (krupuk udang). 11. The input record described the product as '200gm/pack' from Hai Kah Lang, Funan Mall, Singapore; serving size of 200 g is preserved from the input. 12. Halal status is assumed based on typical commercial prawn cracker formulations in Singapore; individual restaurant or brand certification should be verified. 13. trans_fat_g of 0.1 g per 100 g reflects trace levels typical of deep-fried products using partially hydrogenated oils; this may be 0.0 for products using fully refined palm or sunflower oil.
